blocks.content: Ikael could already anticipate the situation.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: This must hurt.
For Wizard, Shirone was not only her sole teacher but also her only source of emotional support.
It's been only seven years since she started experiencing the world. Yet, as a biological entity, her abilities are already exceptional.
And yet, she was capable of love.
Although she lacked the desires of an adult, her feelings were pure and unwavering.
What Shirone provides her is something her parents couldn't. It was inevitable that this kind of bond would form.
For an ordinary child, it might have been something to laugh about and move on from.
But Wizard was no ordinary child. Soon, she would have to stain her hands with the blood of the most evil being in existence: Havitz.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: I hope her emotions remain unchanged. I hope that when she kills Havitz, she is in a state of purity.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Like a machine.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: If thoughts like fighting for someone or being forced into killing seep into her mind…

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: They would create complications.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Shirone must know this. Which means he knowingly accepted the risks.
Shirone could have made Wizard into nothing more than a weapon to kill Havitz.
But he wasn't ruthless. He couldn't bear to ruin a child's life.
It was Yahweh's greatest weakness.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: I have to help.
As an archangel ruling over heaven, Ikael understood the cost of resolution.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: This isn't something he can handle alone. Sometimes… one must be cruel.
Goffin might be angry, but Ikael couldn't watch her son bear humanity's suffering alone anymore.
In the awkward silence on their way to Jive, Wizard suddenly raised her head.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: If Satan dies, will peace come to the world?
Her gaze turned toward Amy.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Well, of course.
Amy replied.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: The chaos in this world is all because of Satan.
Ikael listened quietly.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: But wasn't the world chaotic even before Satan came? I heard there were many wars back then too.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: You heard that from Shirone, didn't you?
Amy understood what Wizard was trying to say.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Yes. Shirone said Satan was born from the human heart. Even if we kill Satan now, if humanity can't form a unified consciousness, the world won't change.
It was Yahweh's philosophy.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: That might be true.
Amy said.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: But we still have to do what we can. Wizard, you'll be the beginning of that change.
Wizard let go of Amy's hand.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: If I kill Satan and peace comes to the world, will you marry Shirone?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: That's something I don't know yet. I love Shirone, but marriage is a matter of the distant future.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: And that's okay?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What?
Wizard furrowed her brows.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: If you love someone, don't you want to be with them all the time? You seem so confident about it.
Amy couldn't explain the trust she had built through countless battlefields with Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: You'll understand when you grow up.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Well, I'm not sure about that. I don't have much time to grow up, after all. I have to kill Satan first to even have a future.
Sensing the weight in Wizard's words, Amy turned to her, only to hear something surprising.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: If I kill Satan, if I succeed in my mission, will you promise me something? That you won't marry Shirone until I'm grown up.
Amy, too, had once been a child. She had done impulsive things in a desperate attempt to gain love from her individualistic family.
Shirone must be her only hope. Her certainty that no one else could be better.
When Amy didn't respond, Wizard continued.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: I was just curious about what you think. Don't take it too seriously. Feelings are…

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Wizard.
Amy stopped walking and crouched down, meeting Wizard's eyes, though Wizard hesitated and looked away.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: I know how much responsibility you're carrying. Your feelings for Shirone, and the fight you're waging for humanity. I'll do anything I can to help you.
The child's lips quivered.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: When Satan is gone from this world, I won't marry Shirone until you've grown up.
Rain fell in Wizard's heart.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: That's not what I meant.
She had dreamed of a way for everyone to be happy, even though she knew it didn't exist.
As tears welled up in Wizard's eyes, Amy smiled.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Let's…
At that moment, a triangular horned Mara, Ashur, appeared beside Ikael, drawing his sword.

blocks.speaker: Ashur
blocks.content: Get down!
Before he finished speaking, Ikael leaped forward.
Boom!
A massive impact struck the earth 23 kilometers from Delta.
Amidst the molten crater, Ikael's group reappeared.
Amy checked on Wizard.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Are you okay?
The child, already in battle mode, was glaring at the towering archangel ahead.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Who is that?
Amy turned to the same direction.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Uriel.
The Archangel of Destruction, Uriel, rose with a powerful vibration that shook the atmosphere.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: Ikael, how long did you think you could keep running from me?
Ikael knew immediately.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: He's resolved herself.
And this was the one situation Ikael had least wanted to face.
Ashur bowed his head.

blocks.speaker: Ashur
blocks.content: I apologize. I blocked all signals that could expose our location, but the narrowed range…
No matter what illusions they used, Ikael's arrival in Jive was inevitable.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: It's not your fault. I share the responsibility for failing to sense his approach.
The fact that Uriel had penetrated even the amplification of their senses showed just how strong he was.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Take Wizard with you. I'll hold him off here.

blocks.speaker: Ashur
blocks.content: But…
Ashur hesitated.

blocks.speaker: Ashur
blocks.content: Can she really win? Uriel has never fought Ikael with his full strength before.
Uriel had always respected Ikael more than any other angel.
As the source of all concepts, Ikael is a force unto herself.
But now, Uriel, the Archangel of Destruction, had decided to break free from that respect and unleash his true power.
Ikael spoke.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: This is a wound I caused. I need to speak with Uriel. Leave this to me.
Ashur bowed his head before materializing beside Wizard using his signal ability.

blocks.speaker: Ashur
blocks.content: Let's go. We'll move to Jive.
With Ikael's stealth compromised, it was only a matter of time before Satiel also discovered her location.
Amy stood up.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: It seems one more will have to stay behind.
As Ashur looked up, another figure in a martial uniform approached decisively.
It was Uriel's second mara, Aruta.
The sole reason for existence lies in the understanding of combat, and his mastery far surpasses that of any human.
Aruta spoke.

blocks.speaker: Aruta
blocks.content: Uriel wishes to face Ikael alone. Anyone who interferes will be eliminated.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Wizard, go.
Amy said, but Wizard shook her head.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: No, I'll do it. I can handle this.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: You can't win. Go.
Wizard was strong, of course, but all her training had been designed with Havitz in mind.
Even with her talent that surpasses experience, she picked the wrong opponent this time. This one is strong.
When asked what kind of strength it was, it would be hard to put into words.
If someone had trained in a single discipline for tens of thousands of years, this is the kind of aura they would emit.
Even a 1% chance that Wizard might fail to complete her mission cannot be allowed.
Wizard was stubborn.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: I can win. If I can't handle someone like this, I won't be able to kill Havitz either.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Don't talk so arrogantly.
The only reason Wizard was unique was that she alone could break through Vanishing.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: You still have a long way to go. Focus on defeating Havitz.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: At least I'm better than you—!
As Wizard began to yell, Amy's Crimson Eye flared up, and she glared at her.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: I said go!
Wizard flinched.
Thunder roared in her heart, and before she could say another word, Ashur activated his Signal.

blocks.speaker: Ashur
blocks.content: Be careful.
he said as his final words before Wizard disappeared.
Amy exhaled deeply.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Don't even think about chasing her.

blocks.speaker: Aruta
blocks.content: Don't misunderstand.
Aruta said.

blocks.speaker: Aruta
blocks.content: This is by Uriel's decision alone. We don't stoop to cowardly tactics.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Is that so?
Amy scoffed.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Big talk, coming here specifically when Shirone isn't around. Was Yahweh that frightening?
Aruta's muscles tensed.

blocks.speaker: Aruta
blocks.content: I'll say it again—
Uriel had said he wanted to talk with Ikael, but explaining further would only diminish their dignity.
Aruta stopped speaking, lowered his stance, and brought his right fist to his side.

blocks.speaker: Aruta
blocks.content: I'll end this in an instant.
In that moment, his aura transformed overwhelmingly.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: That's it.
It felt as if every principle necessary to subdue an opponent with a fist was converging into a single point.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: One-hit kill.
Failure would collapse his stance entirely, but his confidence in not failing was absolute.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Not a good match-up.
The Crimson Eye's ability only peaked after the second exchange, making dodging the first strike critical.

blocks.speaker: Aruta
blocks.content: Your expression doesn't look good. Are you scared?
Amy smirked, lifting the corners of her mouth.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Not at all.
Flames erupted from her body, burning the air as she unleashed the energy of a warrior who would never retreat.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Come at me.
The battle would be over once she dodged and countered his attack, and Aruta knew it.

blocks.speaker: Aruta
blocks.content: Interesting.
Amy's wavering, flame-like aura seemed to follow the natural order itself.
Both of them were aware of the simplest strategy for their confrontation, standing at a tense stalemate.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: Ikael.
Uriel said, rising over the crater.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: I know I am not worthy. But Satiel will destroy humanity. She is not in a state to make sound decisions.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: I know,
Uriel said, landing on the ground and stepping closer to Ikael.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: This isn't what you wanted, was it? You abandoned the angels and heaven for the sake of a single human you loved.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: I didn't abandon them. I simply—
Before she could finish speaking, Uriel's fist slammed into Ikael's face.
A thunderous roar echoed as Ikael was sent flying, carving a V-shaped fissure into the ground.
Standing amidst the radial destruction, Uriel stomped the ground, creating another shockwave as he landed directly before Ikael, who knelt beneath him.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: You could have killed me.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Even just now.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: Since the day you left, I've thought countless times about whether I should erase you completely. The origin of all angels… you.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: It seems you've made up your mind.
Though Ikael still had strength, she remained on the ground, unmoving.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Uriel can destroy everything.
The only reason he had been controlled until now was that Ikael was the very concept from which all angels originated.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: It must feel like destroying part of himself.
This was no mere clash of egos or mutual opposition. It was a single, devastating blow imbued with the intention to truly kill.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: Get up. Let's have a proper duel. Pleading like this won't change my mind.
Uriel said.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: What do you want?
When the two of them clashed, the world would shake, but the outcome wouldn't change.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: I can't let myself be destroyed just yet. At least not until Shirone fulfills his will.
Ikael raised her head.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: If you demand an apology, I will give it. Even more than that, I will do as you wish. Just give me time. I still have work to do here.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: All I do is destroy.
Uriel said, lifting his foot slowly.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: I've thought about this more than I can bear. Since the day I was born, I have thought more deeply than ever…
His expression, as cold as metal, locked onto Ikael's.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: Let's end this.
The Judicial Halo, Ragnarok, activated as his foot hurtled toward Ikael's face.
Boom!
His foot stopped just inches from her nose, but even that motion obliterated the landscape behind her.
Had there been even a flicker of fear in her expression, he would have destroyed her face without hesitation.
But the unwavering eyes that stared back at him, her expression, her poise, and her dignity…

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: Kariel.
She reminded him of a mother.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: I finally understand you.
Confronted by the unique strength only Ikael possessed, Uriel found himself conflicted once more.
